name: Laumė
description: Laumės are enigmatic and complex figures in Lithuanian mythology, often described as female spirits or fairies with supernatural powers. They are known for their beauty, skill in spinning and weaving, and ambiguous nature. While sometimes benevolent, helping with household chores or granting wishes, they can also be mischievous, vengeful, and even dangerous, particularly to those who disrespect them or their craft. Their appearance varies in different accounts, but they are commonly depicted as beautiful women with long hair and sometimes bird-like feet or claws. Laumės are often associated with water sources, forests, and crossroads, and are believed to be active at night. They represent a blend of natural forces, feminine power, and the unpredictable aspects of the supernatural world. Some tales portray them as fallen angels or spirits of the dead, further adding to their multifaceted and mysterious nature. They are a reminder of the importance of respecting the natural world and the powerful forces that lie beyond human understanding. Stories of Laumės serve as cautionary tales and offer insights into the beliefs and values of Lithuanian culture.
subtitle: Lithuanian Fairy of Ambiguous Nature
appearance: Laumės are typically depicted as beautiful women, often with long, flowing hair. Some accounts describe them with bird-like feet or claws instead of human feet. Their clothing is usually simple, often made of linen or other natural materials. Their eyes are said to be mesmerizing and capable of casting spells. Their appearance can vary, sometimes appearing old and withered, and other times young and radiant, depending on their mood or intentions.
habitat: Laumės are often associated with natural environments, particularly near water sources such as rivers, lakes, and springs. They are also found in forests, meadows, and at crossroads. Abandoned mills and bathhouses are also considered to be places where Laumės might reside. These locations are often seen as liminal spaces, connecting the human world with the supernatural realm.
behavior: Laumės are known for their ambiguous behavior. They can be helpful and generous, assisting with household tasks, especially spinning and weaving. However, they can also be mischievous and vengeful, punishing those who offend them or their craft. They are often depicted as tricksters, playing pranks on humans or leading them astray. They are also said to be jealous of human beauty and happiness. Their behavior is unpredictable and often depends on their mood and the respect they receive from humans.

keyMyths.title: Laumė and the Lazy Spinner
keyMyths.description: A Laumė teaches a lazy woman how to spin properly, but punishes her if she doesn't follow her instructions. This tale highlights the importance of hard work and respecting the Laumė's skills.

keyMyths.title: Laumė's Revenge
keyMyths.description: A Laumė is angered when a human steals her spinning wheel or insults her weaving. She seeks revenge, often through curses or misfortune, demonstrating the consequences of disrespecting the supernatural.
